channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fremantle’s Pat Voss fined for unsportsmanlike behaviour towards Melbourne defender Harrison Petty
item.description: Fremantle Dockers forward Patrick Voss has been fined for misconduct following a heated on-field exchange with Melbourne’s Harrison Petty. The AFL’s Match Review Officer found that Voss’s actions — re...

item.title: The Cat: A dark tale of an elderly couple bound by mutual disdain
item.description: Georges Simenon's 'The Cat' (originally 'Le Chat'), published in 1967 and recently translated into English by Ros Schwartz, delves into the life of an elderly couple, Émile and Marguerite, who marry n...

item.title: Truck Drivers Defend Their Vehicle from Armed Carjackers in Melbourne
item.description: Two brave truck drivers fought off armed attackers who attempted to steal their vehicle from a service station car park in Melbourne. The incident occurred in the early hours of the morning, when two ...

item.title: The Growing Role of AI Tokens in Engineering Compensation
item.description: Silicon Valley is experimenting with a new form of compensation for engineers: AI tokens. These tokens are units of compute that can be spent on AI tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini, giving en...

item.title: President Zardari calls on India to honour Indus Waters Treaty commitments
item.description: President Asif Ali Zardari, in his message marking World Water Day 2026, has called upon India to resume full and proper implementation of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) of 1960, stressing that New Del...

item.title: Fremantle's Voss Fined for Taunt of Melbourne's Petty
item.description: Fremantle forward Patrick Voss has been fined $1500 by the AFL for conduct deemed unsportsmanlike following a match against Melbourne. Voss’s actions involved mimicking a crying motion after Melbourne...

item.title: Richard Mercado's ‘Coming Out Perfect’ Explores the Complexities of Growing Up Queer in the Philippines
item.description: Richard Mercado, a Filipino comic artist, has created a young adult graphic novel titled 'Coming Out Perfect,' which delves into the challenges and nuances of growing up queer in the Philippines. The ...

item.title: US-operated Patriot missile likely responsible for Bahrain blast, analysis suggests
item.description: An analysis by academic researchers has suggested that an American-operated Patriot missile was likely behind the explosion in Bahrain's Mahazza neighbourhood on March 9, 2026. The blast, which injure...

item.title: Sri Lanka raises fuel prices amid Middle East conflict and energy concerns
item.description: Sri Lanka has implemented a sharp 25 percent fuel price increase, marking the second rise in just two weeks, as the country braces itself for the economic fallout from the escalating war in the Middle...

item.title: Siberia’s Batagaika Depression Expands as Permafrost Thaws
item.description: The Batagaika ‘crater’ in eastern Siberia is not a meteorite impact site but a massive thermokarst depression created by the collapse of permafrost beneath the surface. Located in the Republic of Sakh...

item.title: Iran Launches Missile Strikes on Israel, Raising Concerns Over Potential Threat to London
item.description: Tehran has launched a fresh wave of missile attacks towards Israel, intensifying fears about the regime's expanding missile capabilities, which may now reach European capitals, including London. Over ...

item.title: Italy: Two Dead, Five Injured in South Tyrol Avalanche
item.description: A tragic avalanche has struck the South Tyrol region of Northern Italy, claiming the lives of two skiers and injuring five others. The incident occurred on Saturday at an altitude of approximately 2,4...

item.title: Lawyer Denies Coercion Claims, Threatens Legal Action Against Businesswoman
item.description: Nigerian human rights lawyer, Marshal Abubakar, has strongly denied claims made by businesswoman Tracy Ohiri, who accused him of coercing her into retracting allegations against Minister of Works, Dav...

item.title: Rising geopolitical tensions and oil prices dim hopes for South African interest rate cuts
item.description: As global geopolitical tensions escalate, particularly in the Middle East, South Africa’s economic outlook has shifted sharply. Economists had once anticipated interest rate cuts in 2026 as inflation ...

item.title: My-ACE China Praises RIRS Efforts to Curb Illegal Tax Collection, Urges Protection for Compliant Businesses in Rivers State
item.description: At the first-ever BusinessDay and Rivers State Internal Revenue Service (RIRS) breakfast meeting held in Port Harcourt with the theme 'Leveraging Nigeria’s Tax Laws to Enhance Profitability', My-ACE C...

item.title: Cuba Faces Second Nationwide Power Outage in a Week Amid Energy Crisis
item.description: Cuba is experiencing a severe energy crisis, marked by a second nationwide power outage in just one week. The blackouts are causing significant disruptions across the country, affecting daily life, ed...

item.title: Impact of West Asia conflict on India-bound flights and aviation sector
item.description: Since the US-Israel war against Iran began on February 28, Indian airlines have faced serious operational challenges. Flights to and from the West now take much longer routes to avoid Iranian and Paki...

item.title: Canadian Federal Government Plans Workforce Reductions Across Departments and Agencies
item.description: The Canadian federal government has announced plans to reduce more than 12,000 full-time equivalent positions across departments and agencies over the next three years as part of the Carney government...

item.title: Kenyan Scholar Dr. Esther Wanja Found Dead in California, Family Seeks Support for Funeral
item.description: Dr Esther Wanja Wachira, a respected scholar and nurse practitioner, has tragically passed away in her apartment in Culver City, California. The news of her death has shocked the Kenyan community, esp...

item.title: Trump issues 48-hour ultimatum to Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz or face strikes on power plants
item.description: US President Donald Trump issued a strong ultimatum on March 21, 2026, stating that if Iran does not fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz without any threats within 48 hours, the United States will strik...
